Scoria Hero Review - Empires and Puzzles

When Scoria crashed into the arena, he did something unprecedented: he stole the Goblins' secret weapon. With Wither, Silence, and a Core that poisons everything it touches, this Construct is a walking disaster zone. But do his stats still hold up against the modern gods?
Scoria: The Magma Golem Who Melted The Goblin Monopoly

When Scoria was released in May 2024, he didn't just arrive; he erupted. As a 5-star Fire hero from the Construct family, he was the trailblazer who finally broke the Goblins' exclusive grip on the "Wither" mechanic. Before him, if you wanted to permanently cripple an enemy's attack, you needed a Goblin. Scoria changed the rules.
Standing as a fiery, jagged golem of molten rock, Scoria brings a kit that is pure aggression: heavy damage, silence, and stat reduction. While he is an

hero, his impact when he fires is comparable to a nuclear meltdown. In the current era of hyper-stats, he has transitioned from an "instant-win button" to a "tactical nuke," but don't let his age fool you—that magma is still hot enough to burn.

Scoria is visually striking, looking like the lovechild of a volcanic eruption and a medieval suit of armor. He is a construct of jagged obsidian and flowing lava, with a skull-like visage that screams "I am not here to negotiate."

The art captures the "Construct" theme perfectly—he isn't born, he is built, and he is built to destroy. The animation of his special skill, "Cauterizing Touch," matches this intensity, delivering a visceral sense of heat and impact. It’s a design that stands out even in a roster filled with gods and monsters.

When Scoria launched, his stats (1046 Attack / 1005 Defense / 1981 Health) were top-tier. However, the game moves fast. Compared to the modern behemoths of late 2025 (who boast 1300+ Attack and 2200+ Health), Scoria's base numbers have undeniably aged.
He is no longer a stat-bully. He can't simply exist on the field and intimidate opponents with his presence alone. To compete in today's Diamond Arena or top-tier Wars, he

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Construct family is famous for its "Cores," and Scoria sports one of the nastiest ones available.
The Construct family bonus isn't the main draw here compared to the Core, but it offers some stat boosts if you run him with teammates like
This is the secret sauce. The core charges up as Scoria takes damage. When he fires:
It scales. If he's taken a beating before firing, the poison is lethal (-13% mana gen per turn stacking is crippling).
He cannot be stopped by "Mindless Attack" or "Silence" (ironic, since he silences others). This ensures that once he is charged, he
A simple but effective boost. For a damage dealer like Scoria, more Attack means harder hits and stronger DoT. It fits his role perfectly.

Upon release, Scoria was widely considered "Overpowered." He was the first non-Goblin to wield Wither, breaking the paywall that locked that mechanic behind the Goblin Village. He was a staple in defenses and a premier offensive weapon.
While his raw stats have been eclipsed, his utility remains S-tier.
is timeless—stat reduction is always good.

He has moved from "God Tier" to "Demi-God." In Rush Wars and Tournaments, he is still a nightmare. In standard play, he serves as a potent flank or wing who can swing a losing battle back in your favor with a single cast.
As of this review, Scoria does not have a costume.

Slightly squishier, risking death before the first cast.
Scoria's job is to hurt people. Wither protects him from return fire, so you can afford to sacrifice some defense for raw power.
. His survivability comes from his Fighter revive and the Wither effect he applies to enemies. You want his special skill to be a devastating blow, not a wet noodle.
Permanently reducing enemy attack (-350) is one of the strongest defensive mechanics in the game, disguised as an offense.
Automatically silencing the highest HP enemy usually neutralizes the biggest threat or the healer keeping the team alive.
The mana generation cut (-13% per turn) forces enemies to ghost tiles or use potions (in PvE) to recover. In PvP, it ruins their timing.
The revive can steal wins from the jaws of defeat.
In a Fast/Very Fast meta, 10 tiles can feel like a marathon. He risks dying before firing.
You cannot choose who gets the 420% hit and Silence. If the highest HP enemy is a passive hero you don't care about, the silence is wasted.
He is significantly behind the 2025 power curve, making him vulnerable to modern snipers who can one-shot him.

Excellent for dismantling bulky defenses. Wither wears them down over time.
The Wither effect makes boss waves manageable, and the silence prevents boss specials.

While Wither and Core effects are often uncleansable (or stack-based), standard status ailments can be washed away. Check the specific interactions, but generally, Scoria sticks well.

Taunt heroes force Scoria to hit them, which might actually be good if they have high HP, but it disrupts his "Hit All" potential if the mechanics interact poorly (though usually, Hit All ignores Taunt for the secondary targets).

If you pulled Scoria, rejoice. He is a cornerstone hero. Prioritize leveling him immediately. He will carry you through Map Stages (Wither makes bosses hit like wet noodles) and will be your ace in Raids. Do not worry about his "aging stats"—for a new player, he is a god.

Scoria is a luxury tool now. He isn't the auto-include he once was. If you have him Maxed/LB1, he's fine. Think twice before committing Alpha Aethers (LB2) unless you use him in your primary War Defense or he is a key part of your Rush lineup. There are harder hitters now, but few offer his specific utility cocktail.
When Scoria dropped, the forums were on fire (pun intended).
— This was the common sentiment. Players were shocked that SG gave such a premium mechanic to a Construct.
— Many compared him to Brimstone, noting the synergy.
"The Silence is the cherry on top."
— Players realized that the damage was good, but the silence on the highest HP hero often clutched games by shutting down a healer about to pop.

Scoria is the definition of a "force multiplier." He doesn't just do damage; he alters the battlefield conditions in your favor. By stripping enemy attack, slowing their mana, and silencing their healthiest member, he dismantles teams systematically.

While Father Time has chipped away at his stat dominance, his kit remains mechanically superior to many "newer, shinier" heroes who offer nothing but big numbers. Scoria is a keeper, a fighter, and a burner. Use him wisely, and watch your enemies crumble into ash.
Still a hot commodity. Keep him off the bench.
Scoria broke the Goblin monopoly on Wither. Is this fiery Construct still a meta-threat in Empires & Puzzles? We analyze his stats, skills, and current viability.
